body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
Previously, we didn't compute the optics when cos_zenith <= 0. This results in NaNs in e.g. aerosol optical depth at night. This PR changes it to always compute optics. Also adds a minimum to cos_zenith when calculating `exp(-τ / cos_zenith)`. Interestingly, when I don't add the clipping, the fluxes are fine for cos_zenith = 1e-10 on cpu, but are Inf on gpu. I'm not sure why. I can do a more extensive test in ClimaAtmos.

This PR also adds a test for these zenith angle edge cases.

title: always calculate optical depth
